summary:
The mesial marginal developmental groove primarily appears on maxillary lateral incisors as confirmed by dental literature focusing on tooth structure anomalies and specific location patterns for these grooves. This feature distinguishes it from other teeth types within the oral cavity based on consistent documentation across multiple reliable resources. To be concise:

The mesial marginal developmental groove typically occurs in maxillary lateral incisors according to dental references highlighting its distinctive presence there compared to other teeth. Key supporting texts confirm this finding specifically targeting structural variations noted predominantly in upper lateral incisors. \(\boxed{\text{Maxillary Lateral Incisors}}\) Based solely on your request, here’s an even more succinct version while still capturing all essential information:

Mesial marginal developmental groves characterize maxillary lateral incisors, per definitive dental studies emphasizing their unique appearance in this particular type of tooth. Thus,

\[ \boxed{\text{maxillary lateral incisors}} \] Summary focuses only on critical details about where mesial marginal developmental grooves appear most commonly found—on maxillary lateral incisors—as stated clearly through referenced medical literature dedicated to describing typical tooth structures including anomalous ones like dens invaginatus or similar terms often interchanged when discussing these formations uniquely present mainly among those front top permanent canine predecessors (\( \boxed{\text{maxillary lateral incisors}} \)).
